source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Warp is an engineering-driven company building the future of automated back-office operations. We're looking for a growth engineer who's excited to join our early story and help us dominate the market.



As a Growth Engineer at Warp, you'll sit at the critical intersection of engineering, data, and growth strategy. You'll ship high-impact metrics-driven technical projects that directly accelerate our mission to eliminate HR operations overhead for startups forever. Working cross-functionally with engineering, sales, and growth teams, you'll build the data infrastructure, automation tools, and analytical capabilities that power our rapid expansion.



This role offers the rare opportunity to solve complex technical challenges while seeing immediate business impact. You'll architect the revenue ops systems that streamline our sales processes, build growth tooling that accelerates customer acquisition, and create the technical infrastructure that empowers our sales and marketing teams with better data, automation, and insights.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Growth Experimentation and Optimization: Create and iterate on product and marketing technical projects to help Warp grow 10x

 - Data Analysis and Product Knowledge: Analyze growth metrics to inform sales, marketing, and product teams

 - Platform Engineering: Build robust platform features, integrations, and dashboards combining existing Warp products and third-party data

 - Cross Functional Collaboration: Work with sales, growth, product and engineering to identify, specify, document, and build

 - Rev Ops: build and ship internal tools related to billing and revenue recognition
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of software engineering experience

 - Startup experience as a founder, early engineer, or growth roles

 - An understanding of B2B Marketing and Sales metrics and processes

 - Strong expertise in programming

 - Excellent communication skills

 - High agency and the ability to figure things out




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:

If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a SDR team with clear paths to AE, sales leadership, or other growth areas based on performance and interest. You'll join our SDR team, building the outbound engine that will fuel Warp's growth from $XM to $XXM+ ARR.

We're hiring exceptional humans who happen to be interested in sales, not traditional salespeople. Our best performers come from investment banking, teaching, consulting, top-tier customer success, or have been founders who've done their own sales - anywhere excellence and grit intersect.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Generate qualified meetings per month through strategic outbound

 - Build and iterate on outreach sequences that convert founders

 - Identify ICP patterns and document winning playbooks

 - Experiment with new channels (complete freedom to innovate)

 - Maintain and log all sales touch points within Attio

 - Build repeatable systems that can scale to 10+ SDRs




WHAT YOU NEED

We don’t care what school you went and what you majored in. What we care about is how driven you are to succeed in this role.

 - 1+ year of excellence in any demanding role (sales, banking, consulting, teaching, etc.)

 - Track record of self-directed learning and rapid skill acquisition

 - Clear communicator - written, verbal, and over video

 - Ability to speak credibly to technical founders

 - Thrives in a startup setting: resourceful, adaptable, and self-directed

 - High sense of urgency and ability to work within undefined processes

Who Thrives Here:

 - Former athletes who miss competing

 - People who've built something from nothing

 - Self-taught experts in any domain

 - Those who see rejection as data, not defeat

 - Willingness and openness to learn new things on a daily basis

Most Important Qualities:

 - Figure-it-out factor: You see ambiguity as opportunity, not obstacle

 - Competitive drive: You keep score and hate losing more than you love winning

 - Coachability: You seek feedback actively and implement it immediately




NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience at a startup

 - Network within the startup/tech community

 - Hands-on experience with sales tools (Lemlist, Atto, or similar)




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we pri

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring an account executive who will directly work within the sales team. You will be the point of contact for companies looking to use Warp and are responsible for sourcing, conducting product demos, following up with them, and ensuring they receive the best resources to make an informed decision about using Warp.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the end-to-end sales process from outbound prospecting to discovery, demoing Warp, and closing deals.

 - Consistently source and qualify new leads.

 - Follow up, close, and hand off customers to the customer success team

 - Collaborate with SDR's, Marketing and Product to create a smooth buying experience.

 - Maintain and log all sales touch points within Salesforce
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - High level of comfort with sales tools and processes

 - Experience handling a full-cycle sales process, from outbounding, to closing

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Proven track record of meeting and exceeding sales targets

 - High sense of urgency and ability to work within undefined processes

 - Willingness and openness to learn new things on a daily basis

 - 1-year of selling experience
   
   


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience in fast sales cycles

 - Experience at a startup

 - Experience in selling Payroll/Compliance products




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.

ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Software Engineer on the Product team, you'll build the features that make Warp indispensable. This isn't a siloed frontend or backend role. You'll work full-stack across our entire product surface, interface directly with customers, spec features alongside the team, and ship things that matter. One week you're building HRIS workflows, the next you're wiring up an AI agent that automates something a customer used to do manually.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and own product features across Warp's full surface: HRIS, payroll, benefits, IT, Warp AI agents, and more

 - Work full-stack: schema design, backend services, and polished frontends

 - Interface directly with customers and the sales team to understand pain points and turn them into product

 - Collaborate directly with the CTO and CEO

 - Help us scale 10x in the next year
   
   


YOU'LL BE GOOD FOR THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Are product-minded with a strong eye for user experience

 - Love programming and obsess over details

 - Can move fast without leaving a mess

 - Are comfortable talking to customers and translating feedback into product

 - Have shipped features that real users rely on




WHAT YOU NEED

 - Experience building full-stack applications

 - Strong expertise in a modern, strongly typed programming language

 - Experience designing and building reliable backend systems

 - High agency. You figure things out.

 - Willingness to work from our NYC office five days a week




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

source_jobs.title: Tax Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a detail-oriented Tax Operations Specialist to join our payroll tax compliance team. You'll directly report to our Tax Operations Lead, becoming the first line of defense when customers receive payroll tax notices. This role combines technical tax expertise with exceptional customer service - you'll investigate issues, communicate with tax agencies, and guide founders through resolutions with clarity and confidence.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone with 2-4 years of payroll tax experience who wants to deepen their multi-state compliance expertise while helping build scalable processes at a fast-growing startup.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Notice Resolution & Customer Support: Decode federal, state, and local payroll tax notices, translate tax jargon into plain English, and guide founders through resolution via email, Zoom, and Pylon

 - Agency Communication: Draft letters, file responses, and represent Warp in conversations with IRS, state, and local tax agencies to resolve account discrepancies, penalties, and registration issues

 - Tax Operations: Process state tax registrations, assist with jurisdiction setup across all 50 states, and support quarterly filings (Form 941 and state equivalents)

 - Documentation & Tracking: Maintain detailed case notes in Linear and Notion, track tax issues using project management platforms, and perform tax reconciliations

 - Process Improvement: Flag compliance trends and process gaps, turn recurring issues into FAQs and Founder Guides, and help improve workflows through automation initiatives

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Customer Support, Engineering, Product, and third-party administrators to test new tools and address client tax questions

 - Year-End Support: Assist with end of year preparation and other annual compliance processes
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field (or equivalent experience)

 - 2-4 years of payroll or payroll tax experience

 - Strong attention to detail and exceptional organizational skills - you rigorously track every deadline, document, and follow-up

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills with ability to translate complex tax concepts for non-technical audiences

 - Comfort juggling high notice volume and shifting priorities while maintaining exceptional customer experience

 - Experience with project management tools (Jira, Linear) and collaboration platforms (Slack, Zoom, Notion)

 - Proficiency with Google Sheets for data analysis and reconciliation

 - Experience with Mastertax or similar tax filing platforms

 - Comfortable making phone calls to tax agencies and researching compliance requirements

 - Willingness and openness to learn new things daily
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Multi-state payro

source_jobs.title: Customer Experience Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Customer Experience Specialist at Warp, you'll be the frontline advocate for our customers and a deep product expert on the Warp platform. You'll join our growing Customer Experience team — reporting to the Head of Customer Success — and serve as the go-to person when customers need guidance, answers, or hands-on support. This isn't a ticket-submitting role. You'll be a true partner in resolving customer cases end-to-end, translating real customer pain into actionable insights for our engineering and product teams, and getting ahead of issues before they become patterns.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own real-time customer engagement via chat, Slack, and email — maintaining high service quality and fast response times

 - Resolve complex payroll challenges including tax notices and payment issues

 - Develop deep product expertise in Warp and serve as the team's internal resource for how the platform works

 - Partner with engineering on customer cases — context-setting, reproducing issues, and seeing them through to resolution rather than simply escalating

 - Translate customer-reported issues into clear, well-documented technical briefs that engineering and product can act on

 - Identify recurring or deep-rooted issues and bring them to leadership with recommendations — shifting from reactive resolution to proactive prevention

 - Communicate with third-party partners integrated with Warp on behalf of customers

 - Build and maintain a customer-facing knowledge base that evolves with product rollouts and common queries
   
   


YOU WILL BE GOOD FOR THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Are genuinely curious about how products work and motivated to become a subject matter expert

 - Can translate complex or technical issues into plain language — for customers and internal teams alike

 - Spot patterns in customer feedback and think about root causes, not just quick fixes

 - Take ownership of customer cases from first touch to full resolution

 - Thrive in a fast-paced environment where processes are still being built
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - 1–2+ years of customer support experience, ideally in a technical or product-forward environment

 - Strong documentation and follow-up skills with meticulous attention to detail

 - High customer empathy paired with sharp problem-solving instincts

 - Experience with tools like Linear, Pylon, or Slack — or ability to learn quickly

 - Ability to manage multiple cases simultaneously without letting anything fall through the cracks

 - Nice to have: background in HR, payroll, or financial services




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take th

source_jobs.title: Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Warp is a product-driven company building the future of automated back-office operations. We're looking for a design engineer who's excited to join our early story and help us maintain the highest quality bar possible.



As a Design Engineer at Warp, you'll be directly responsible for curating the user experience of the next generation of fintech products. You'll design core cross-platform product flows, landing pages, and marketing assets that convert prospects into customers and transform complex financial operations into seamless user journeys. Working at the intersection of design and engineering, you'll prototype user-experiences, establish design patterns that scale across our platform, and implement frontend experiences that measurably increase user satisfaction.



This role offers the rare opportunity to shape how customers experience financial technology while driving measurable business outcomes. You'll design and ship the user interfaces that make complex financial workflows intuitive, create design systems that scale across our product suite, and develop the frontend infrastructure that transforms product insights into delightful experiences.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Core Product Design and Development: Design, build, and maintain core product design systems across web and mobile applications, ensuring consistent user experiences that scale with our rapid growth

 - Growth and Marketing Asset Design: Design social media assets, landing pages, event posters, and team merchandise that drive attention and growth while maintaining brand consistency across all touchpoints

 - Design System Evolution: Establish and maintain component libraries, design tokens, and documentation that enable consistent, efficient product development across the entire platform

 - Prototyping and Validation: Use design software and AI to rapidly prototype new features and interface concepts to validate design decisions before full implementation

 - Process and Tooling: Build internal design and development workflows that improve team velocity, from design handoff processes to automated testing for visual consistency

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner directly with product, engineering, and sales teams to translate business requirements into elegant design solutions that balance user needs with technical constraints

 - Strategic Design Thinking: Contribute to long-term product vision by identifying opportunities to simplify complex financial workflows and reduce operational overhead for our users




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of product design and engineering experience

 - Strong expertise in product design software (pref. Figma)

 - Experience writing CSS

 - Startup or digital agency experience as a founder, early engineer, or design roles

 - An understanding of fintech product

source_jobs.description:

Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring an Account Manager to own strategic customer relationships and drive measurable business outcomes across a portfolio of accounts. You'll ensure customers maximize value from our payroll and tax solutions while identifying opportunities for growth and expansion. You'll serve as the trusted advisor to your customers, combining deep product expertise with business acumen to influence their success and drive retention.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Drive revenue across your book of business by owning retention, expansion, and growth.

 - Hit and exceed a quarterly expansion target by taking deals from opportunity identification through signed contract

 - Run structured QBRs with customers to review health metrics, surface expansion opportunities, and advance active deals

 - Proactively identify churn risk through health monitoring and execute targeted interventions to protect gross retention

 - Develop a deep understanding of customer business needs to deliver consultative guidance that ties Warp's product suite to their operational goals

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Support, Product, and Engineering to resolve complex issues and advocate for customer priorities
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Minimum 2-3 years of new business sales or account management experience in a B2B organization

 - Proven track record managing a book of business and driving retention, expansion, and customer satisfaction

 - Strong business acumen with the ability to anticipate customer needs, identify growth opportunities, and tie product usage to business outcomes

 - Experience running executive-level business reviews and presenting data-driven recommendations

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels

 - Highly organized, with the ability to prioritize strategically across a large portfolio while meeting targets

 - High adaptability and comfort with the pace and change of an early-stage startup

 


NICE TO HAVES

 - Background working with early-stage startup customers or in high-growth B2B SaaS

 - Experience building customer success processes and playbooks from the ground up

 - Track record of consistently exceeding retention and expansion targets

 - Technical aptitude with ability to quickly learn complex products







BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep,

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer, Warp Insurance Services
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Warp Insurance Services is the in-house health insurance brokerage built inside Warp, the AI-native payroll, HRIS, and benefits platform. We broker medical, dental, vision, and ancillary coverage directly for Warp's customers, no outside broker required.

We are hiring our first GTM Engineer. This is not a traditional account executive seat. You will build and run the WIS go-to-market motion end to end: converting existing Warp payroll clients into WIS benefits clients, driving broker-of-record transfers, standing up new sourcing channels through insurance brokers and CPAs, and owning delivery against an aggressive covered-employee onboarding target.

You will carry a number, but you will also design the system that hits it. If you want to own a channel rather than inherit a territory, this is the role.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the full WIS sales funnel from sourcing through close and onboarding handoff

 - Convert existing Warp payroll clients into WIS benefits clients through structured upsell motions

 - Run broker-of-record transfers, coordinating with service and ops on clean transitions

 - Build the broker channel from scratch: recruit, enable, and manage referring brokers

 - Build the CPA channel: establish referral relationships with accounting firms whose clients need group benefits

 - Partner with Warp core sales reps so they proactively identify and start WIS leads, and own the handoff mechanics on both sides

 - Own onboarding delivery against the covered-employee target, working with the WIS service team on enrollment execution

 - Build the operational infrastructure for the motion in the existing stack (Pylon, Linear, Salesforce, Employee Navigator), not abstract playbooks
   
    


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years in benefits, insurance, or B2B sales, with real exposure to group health

 - A builder. You have stood up a channel, a partnership motion, or a GTM process from zero, not just worked an assigned book

 - Comfortable orchestrating other teams (core sales, service) rather than working alone

 - Fluent in health insurance mechanics: BOR, carrier relationships, group eligibility, enrollment

 - Systems-minded. You map work to tools and workflows and you keep a clean pipeline

 - Clear, human communicator. No jargon fog with brokers, CPAs, or clients




NICE TO HAVE

 - Existing broker or CPA relationships you can activate

 - Experience with a licensed brokerage or GA

 - Startup GTM experience at an early, build-it-yourself stage




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibili

If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Customer Activations Manager to own the entire customer journey from signed contract through onboarding graduation. You'll report to the Head of Customer Success. Every new customer onboarding flows through this role — which means you'll directly shape the first experience every Warp customer has with the product.

A customer isn't done onboarding when their first payroll runs. They're done when they're genuinely set up to succeed: the right stakeholders are engaged, the platform is configured for their setup, and they've seen the value Warp delivers. That bar is higher than most onboarding roles — and that's intentional.

At its core, this is a builder role. You'll run structured implementation tracks across small startups and 200+ person companies at the same time, calibrate your approach by company size, and work cross-functionally with Engineering, Tax Ops, and the broader Customer Success team to make sure no customer falls through the cracks between signed and live. The playbooks exist because someone wrote them — and you'll be the one who makes them better.

You'll have a direct line to the Head of Customer Success and own the KPIs that define activation: time to first payroll, onboarding graduation rate, and a 90%+ activation target across your book. Variable comp is tied directly to hitting them.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 

 - Own every new customer onboarding end-to-end from signed contract through graduation, with clean handoff criteria to Account Management.

 - Execute tiered onboarding tracks calibrated to company size: lightweight self-serve oversight for small accounts, structured multi-stakeholder implementations for mid-market and enterprise.

 - Drive first payroll on the first eligible pay cycle ensuring data migration accuracy, tax registrations, and bank verification are complete before a customer's next pay period closes.

 - Certify graduation: not just a payroll run, but confirmed stakeholder engagement, post-go-live training, and feature adoption appropriate to the customer's setup.

 - Manage a portfolio of concurrent implementations with precision: proactive outreach, clear timelines, and zero dropped handoffs.

 - Execute against tier-specific templates, escalation paths, and graduation checklists and flag where they need to be improved.

 - Partner cross-functionally: Engineering for custom setups, Tax Ops on state registration dependencies, Support on go-live blockers.

 - Feed structured product feedback from implementation directly into the product team.

 - Own the playbook itself, continuously improving the templates, escalation paths, and graduation criteria you run against.
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - At least 3+ years of experience in implementation, customer success, or project management in a B2B SaaS organization with a track record o

source_jobs.title: Senior Customer Experience Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Most support orgs are inherited. This one gets built. You'll be the first Senior CX Manager at Warp, the person who decides what great looks like, builds the team that delivers it, and owns the outcome completely. This is a role for someone who has done this before, moves fast, and wants to build something they're proud of at a company that's rewriting how payroll works.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

The support org is yours — the people, the process, the metrics, the culture, and the escalations. You'll set the standard, hire to it, and hold the team to it. You'll develop managers and specialists, build the career pathing and onboarding infrastructure that scales with us, and create a culture of accountability that's grounded in data and driven by outcomes.

When a payroll issue is complex, high-stakes, or crossing multiple teams, it lands with you. You'll drive resolution end-to-end, orchestrating across product, engineering, tax, and benefits with the kind of control and clarity that makes customers trust us and internal teams respect you. You're not just solving problems — you're building the systems, runbooks, SLA frameworks, and escalation playbooks that prevent them. You'll conduct root cause analysis, surface trends to leadership, and partner with Product and Engineering to close the loop on anything systemic. You are the voice of the customer at the table and you show up that way every time.




WHAT YOU NEED

You have led and managed support teams — not informally, not as a senior IC. You've owned the headcount, the SLAs, the performance reviews, and the structure. You've built things from scratch and have the track record to prove it. Managing managers or team leads is a strong plus; we're building a function that will need that layer and we want someone who's ready for it.

You have 5–8 years in support operations or a customer-facing leadership role with meaningful time at a fintech, HRIS, or payroll platform. Your payroll knowledge is deep — you can debug a tax filing error, untangle a multi-state compliance issue, and explain garnishments to a first-time HR admin without breaking a sweat. You know how to hold people accountable without losing them. You don't confuse activity with results.

You communicate with precision at every level. You can de-escalate a furious customer and brief a CEO on the same issue within the hour. You stay composed when things are on fire.

You are data-fluent, product-minded, and relentlessly biased toward action. You don't wait for a playbook. You write it.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Familiarity with HRIS systems, accounting software (e.g. QuickBooks, Xero), or payroll platforms.

 - Experience at an early-stage or high-growth company where the playbook was still being written.

 - Track record owning implementation metrics end-to-end with specific numbers

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Payments
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Software Engineer focused on Payments at Warp, you'll build and own the financial infrastructure that powers Warp. Core ledgering, US and global payment rails, payroll engine, earnings calculations, tax engines, and the compliance layer around all of it. This is deeply technical work with real financial stakes. Very few engineers get to work on problems like this, at this scale, this early. You'll work directly with the CTO and have outsized influence over how we architect one of the most critical systems in the company.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own and scale Warp's core ledgering and payments infrastructure

 - Develop and own the payroll engine, earnings engine, and tax engine

 - Architect US and global payment rails for domestic and international payroll

 - Build fault-tolerant, auditable financial systems designed to process billions of dollars

 - Define the payments architecture roadmap in collaboration with the CTO




YOU'LL BE GOOD FOR THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Have deep experience in payments, fintech, or financial infrastructure

 - Care intensely about correctness, auditability, and reliability

 - Have owned payments or fintech infrastructure at a high-growth startup

 - Understand the unique challenges of financial systems: idempotency, reconciliation, compliance

 - Love hard infrastructure problems with direct business impact




WHAT YOU NEED

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience

 - 2+ years working on payments, fintech, or core financial infrastructure

 - Experience building highly reliable, fault-tolerant distributed systems

 - Strong expertise in strongly typed languages

 - Deep understanding of financial systems and their unique constraints

 - Willingness to work from our NYC office five days a week




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Identity & Access
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.

If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Software Engineer focused on Identity & Access at Warp, you'll build foundational authentication and authorization systems, role-based access controls, application provisioning, and cross-platform MDM. You're building the core infrastructure that controls how thousands of companies manage who has access to what, on which devices, from day one of employment to the last. It's high-stakes, and it touches every part of the product.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build foundational authentication and authorization infrastructure across Warp's entire product

 - Design and implement role-based and attribute-based access control systems

 - Build application provisioning and deprovisioning, automatically granting and revoking access as employees join, change roles, or leave

 - Build cross-platform MDM for macOS, Windows, iOS, and Android

 - Own the protocols that power it all: SAML, SCIM, OIDC, OAuth

 - Work directly with customers to define what automated IT management actually looks like at scale
   
   


YOU'LL BE GOOD FOR THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Have built authentication or authorization systems. Not just configured them.

 - Understand access control models (RBAC, ABAC) and how to implement them at scale

 - Have experience with cross-platform device management or MDM

 - Have owned identity or access infrastructure at a high-growth startup or enterprise software company

 - Get energized by technically hard problems with serious security implications
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience

 - Deep familiarity with identity protocols: SAML, SCIM, OIDC, OAuth

 - Experience building or owning MDM or cross-platform device management

 - Strong systems-level thinking and security-first mindset

 - Experience designing access control systems at scale

 - Willingness to work from our NYC office five days a week




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

ABOUT WARP

Warp is building self-driving employee management. Payroll, tax compliance across thousands of jurisdictions, benefits, IT, and HRIS that runs autonomously so companies never have to think about back-office operations again.

We process hundreds of millions in payments across 1,000+ companies. Revenue has grown 5x+ YoY for two years. We're a team of ~40 in NYC in Flatiron, scaling fast, and expecting to be 100 people by end of year.

The incumbents (ADP, Paylocity, Paychex) built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Even the modern players are bolting chatbots onto legacy systems. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.

We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

Backed by $25M+ from Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Software Engineer focused on Developer Platform at Warp, you'll build the external-facing API layer that lets companies programmatically control payroll, benefits, HR, IT, and more. That means designing robust APIs, building OAuth flows and authentication infrastructure, webhook delivery systems, event log infrastructure and the products that make them queryable, SDK generation, and world-class documentation. You'll own the full developer experience, from the reliability of the underlying platform to the clarity of the docs, and be the primary interface between Warp's core systems and the developers who build on top of them.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, build, and maintain Warp's external APIs

 - Build webhook infrastructure: reliable delivery, retries, failure visibility

 - Build event log systems and the products that make them queryable by customers and partners

 - Build OAuth and authentication infrastructure for third-party integrations

 - Own SDK generation and developer tooling

 - Write and maintain developer documentation

 - Handle technical communications with integration partners and enterprise customers

 - Define the API platform roadmap in collaboration with the CTO
   
   


YOU'LL BE GOOD FOR THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Have built developer-facing APIs and platforms that external developers rely on

 - Have experience building webhook systems, event streaming, or audit log infrastructure

 - Care deeply about API ergonomics, consistency, and documentation quality

 - Have owned a developer platform or external API at a

source_jobs.title: Head of Finance
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Head of Finance to build and own the financial engine of a company scaling revenue to $50M+. You'll be the CEO's primary partner on strategic finance: the operating model, board and investor reporting, unit economics, and the capital decisions that come with an $85M-raised, money-moving business. You'll start hands-on and build the team beneath you over time.



What you'll do

 - Own the financial model and forecast. Turn the charter's revenue trajectory into a plan the company runs against and a model the board trusts.

 - Run board and investor reporting: monthly metrics, quarterly board materials, and the financial narrative behind both.

 - Own unit economics and pricing analysis. ARPU, gross margin, payback, and the economics of new lines like benefits brokerage and in-house payroll rails.

 - Partner on capital allocation. Where the Series B goes, how we think about burn and runway, and the return on big bets like replacing our money-movement vendor.

 - Build the finance function. Hire and lead the controller and ops side, and own relationships with our wealth, banking, and accounting partners and our auditors.

 - Be the financial backbone of the next fundraise when it comes.



What we're looking for:

 - 5+ years in finance, including ownership of FP&A and board reporting at a high-growth startup.

 - Deep FP&A and strategic finance ability. You can build a model yourself from scratch and defend all assumptions to a board.

 - You are fluent in SaaS metrics, and ideally fintech or payments economics: float, money movement, and multi-product ARPU.

 - You've partnered directly with a founder or CEO to make decisions and build.

 - You can be hands-on now, and a team-builder later. No task is beneath our leadership at this stage.



Nice to Have:

 - Experience in the payroll, payments, insurance space, or another regulated money-moving business.

 - You've helped run a priced fundraise.




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

source_jobs.description:

We're hiring our first Controller to own the books and financial operations of a company that moves real money every day. You'll run accounting, close, tax, and the controls a payroll and money-movement business depends on. Hands-on IC role reporting to the Head of Finance.



What you'll do

 - Own the monthly close and keep the books clean and audit-ready.

 - Run accounting operations: AP, AR, billing reconciliation, and revenue recognition as we migrate billing to ACH.

 - Own corporate tax and compliance filings, working with our outside tax partners.

 - Reconcile money movement. We process tens of millions in monthly volume across multiple rails, soon including our own. The numbers tie out, every time.

 - Build financial controls and processes that scale from 30 to 70+ people without adding chaos.

 - Partner with the Head of Finance on reporting and with Ops on tax compliance.



What we're looking for

 - 5+ years in accounting and controllership, ideally including a high-growth startup.

 - CPA or equivalent rigor. You care about getting the details exactly right.

 - Hands-on with close, reconciliation, and accounting systems.

 - Comfortable in a company where money movement is core to the product, not a side function.

 - High ownership and autonomy. You see a broken process and fix it.



Nice to Have:

 - Payroll, payments, or fintech background.

 - You've helped a company prep for its first formal audit.




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

We're hiring a Business Operations generalist to work directly with the CEO and exec team on whatever matters most right now. You'll move across GTM, RevOps, finance, and ops, taking ambiguous, high-impact problems and turning them into results. This is the role for someone who wants broad, cross-functional exposure at the center of a company scaling fast post-Series B.



What you'll do

 - Drive execution on the company's most important initiatives, often the ones that don't fit neatly on any single team.

 - Own and improve operating processes as we scale from 30 to 70+ people, before they break.

 - Support RevOps and our Salesforce migration: pipeline reporting, routing, and the systems that keep the sales engine running.

 - Run the analysis behind real decisions: pricing and packaging, unit economics, and vendor evaluations across benefits, IT, and infrastructure.

 - Help prepare board and investor materials alongside the CEO and Head of Finance.

 - Select and onboard new vendors, and project-manage cross-functional rollouts end to end.



What we're looking for

 - 3-5 years in management consulting (MBB preferred), venture capital, private equity, or business operations at a high-growth startup.

 - Highly organized, adaptable, and a strong problem-solver who is at home in ambiguity.

 - Bias toward execution. You move quickly, take ownership, and pitch in well beyond your lane.

 - Sharp analytically. Equally comfortable in a spreadsheet and in front of the exec team.

 - Excellent written communication.



Nice to Have

 - Fintech, payroll, or B2B SaaS exposure.

 - You've built the processes that carried a company through our stage of scale.




BENEFITS AT WARP:

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.



We're hiring a Head of Demand Generation to own the top of Warp's funnel and turn our Series B into pipeline. You'll own how ambitious companies discover Warp and decide to talk to us: paid, lifecycle, web conversion, and the demand programs that feed a sales team scaling from a handful of AEs to 20+. You'll partner closely with our Head of Marketing on brand and with our Head of Sales on conversion.



What you'll do

 - Own pipeline creation as a number. Marketing-sourced qualified pipeline is your scoreboard, at 3-4x coverage on the bookings target.

 - Run paid acquisition across channels with a real bar for CAC and payback.

 - Own website conversion: traffic to demo, the funnel math, and the experiments that move it.

 - Build lifecycle and nurture programs that turn inbound interest into qualified demos.

 - Partner with sales and RevOps on attribution, routing, and the handoff that decides whether a lead becomes revenue.

 - Build the demand gen function as spend scales post-Series B.



What we're looking for

 - 6+ years in demand gen or growth marketing at a B2B SaaS company, including scaling pipeline through a growth stage.

 - You think in funnels and own a number. You can tie spend to pipeline to revenue.

 - Hands-on operator who can run the channels yourself now and hire as you grow.

 - Strong partnership instincts with sales. You measure yourself on revenue, not leads.



Nice to Have

 - Experience marketing to founders, finance, or people and ops buyers.

 - Fintech, HR tech, or developer-adjacent background.




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.



We're hiring our first Product Marketing Manager to own the story behind a multi-product platform taking on Workday and the legacy payroll incumbents. You'll connect what engineering ships to how the market understands it: positioning, messaging, launches, competitive intelligence, and the enablement that helps sales win. High-ownership IC role with room to build the function.



What you'll do

 - Own positioning and messaging across the platform: payroll, benefits, IT, and everything we add.

 - Drive product launches end to end. The Series B announcement and the launches behind it are an early, visible test.

 - Own competitive intelligence. Know Workday, Rippling, Gusto, and the incumbents cold, and arm sales accordingly.

 - Build sales enablement: pitch narratives, battlecards, one-pagers, and the materials that close enterprise deals.

 - Own how we communicate pricing and packaging as we roll out new tiers and add-ons.

 - Be the voice of the customer in product decisions and the voice of product in the market.



What we're looking for

 - 4+ years in product marketing at a B2B SaaS company, ideally with a multi-product or platform story.

 - You write exceptionally well and can make a complex product feel simple and inevitable.

 - You've owned launches and built enablement that sales actually used.

 - Comfortable going deep on a technical, regulated product.



Nice to have

 - Fintech, HR tech, or payroll and payments background.

 - You've positioned a challenger against an entrenched incumbent.




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT WARP

Warp is the AI-native Employee Management Platform for ambitious companies. Our platform includes HRIS, payroll, IT, benefits, tax compliance, and more - all built from scratch on an AI-native architecture from day 1.



Warp embeds itself into every part of a company's people operating layer, we process over half a billion $ annually in payroll, on track to grow to $2B.


Legacy vendors in our space built their businesses by profiting from complexity. Warp is built from day one for AI to be the primary operator, not a feature. Every advance in LLM capability widens our lead.


We hire people who ship constantly, own outcomes, and care about craft. We're in the office five days a week because speed and proximity are a competitive advantage at this stage. If you want to build something that automates an entire category of core work, not just another point solution, this is the place.

We've raised over $85M from top investors like Ashton Kutcher/Sound Ventures, Y Combinator, founders of Dropbox, Replit, SV Angel, and Homebrew Capital. Built by an obsessive team from MIT, Ramp, Brex, Google, Dropbox, Apple, and more.



We're hiring a Senior Technical Recruiter to build the engineering, product, and design team behind Warp's next phase. We're going from 30+ to 70+ this year, and the hardest, highest-leverage part of that is technical hiring. This role is for someone who treats sourcing as a craft: you know where the best engineers are, how to get their attention, and how to close them. You'll own technical hiring strategy end to end, working with our recruiting lead on ops and partnering directly with our CTO and engineering leads on the bar.



What you'll do

 - Build and own sourcing strategy for engineering, product, and design, from target-list construction to first outreach to close.

 - Hunt for top-tier talent for our hardest roles: payments and infra engineers for the money-movement rails, identity and access, developer platform, and design engineering.

 - Run full-cycle recruiting for complex technical roles, coordinating interviews, technical assessments, and system design, with a candidate experience that makes people want in.

 - Be the market intelligence resource for hiring managers: comp benchmarking, competitive landscape, and talent-pool sizing.

 - Run a fast, high-bar process. We move at warp speed and great candidates feel it.



What we're looking for

 - 5+ years in technical recruiting, with a heavy emphasis on outbound sourcing.

 - Strong opinions on what a great engineer looks like, and the examples to defend them.

 - A track record driving searches end to end across a wide range of engineering roles, flagging concerns early and keeping them on track.

 - Cold outreach that converts. You can get a response from someone who wasn't looking.

 - You use AI throughout your workflow.

 - High ownership and high standards. You'd rather leave a seat open than fill it wrong.



Nice to have

 - You've hired for fintech, infra, or payments teams.

 - Experience building an in-person, high-intensity team.




BENEFITS AT WARP:

 - 100% Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents.

 - 401(k) Matching: Start investing in your future from day one.

 - Unlimited PTO: We offer flexible PTO, meaning you’re trusted to take the time you need to recharge while balancing your responsibilities and team commitments.

 - Commuter Benefits: Pre-tax commuter benefits for your daily trip to our NYC office.

 - Catered Lunch Daily: Great food, every day, on us. No meal prep, no decision fatigue.

 - Quarterly Offsites: Beautiful locations to connect as a team. Past locations include Spain and Catskills.

Warp is based in New York City, and we prioritize candidates who can commit to being on-site 5x per week. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

notes: High volume of engineering and product roles, with a strong focus on AI and software development. Some GTM roles present, but engineering core remains dominant.
